req: REQ-030 (Operations, Incident Response & Admin Correction Tooling)

issues.detail: The clause “a silently-failed job or money-path bug is caught by the daily read or a user report” is only half covered: the test and coverage map omit the user-report detection path.
issues.fix: Add an observable documentation/procedure check that user reports are a recognized detection input routed into the first-class incident workflow, without requiring automated intake unless specified.

issues.detail: “Backup restore within the 4-hour objective” was narrowed to a runbook merely “stating the 4-hour objective.” A nonfunctional restore procedure could pass.
issues.fix: Execute a restore drill from a known backup and assert successful restoration within four hours; retain the runbook-content check separately.

issues.detail: The [cx] note claims balanced corrections are asserted for every divergence class, but AT-030.07 asserts only authority conformance, source reference, and dashboard visibility. Only AT-030.06 checks balance, for the top-up fixture, so “every correction is balanced” remains partially uncovered.
issues.fix: For the chargeback, Anthropic-spend, and internal-pairing fixtures, assert independently that each resulting correction is balanced.

issues.detail: Requiring exactly one audit record/event exceeds “audited.” A compliant implementation may retain multiple immutable audit records or record the rejected replay attempt while still producing exactly one correction posting.
issues.fix: Require at least one retrievable immutable audit record for the successful correction and exactly one correction posting after replay; allow separate audit records for attempts or processing stages.

issues.detail: “NO approval queue and NO pending-approval state exists anywhere” is unqualified and contradicts REQ-005.5/REQ-023, which require the founder’s triage review queue and pending human decision before publication.
issues.fix: Scope the absence probe to money corrections: no correction-approval queue and no correction pending-approval state anywhere; do not prohibit unrelated PRD-required approval workflows.
